heading: Assessment
html:
Assess for emergency complications, cancer risk pattern, and menopausal status at first contact.

  • Urgently assess severe acute unilateral pain, peritonism, haemodynamic instability, fever, or persistent vomiting.
  • Elicit ovarian-cancer symptom pattern: persistent bloating, early satiety/loss of appetite, pelvic or abdominal pain, urinary urgency/frequency.
  • Examine for abdominal/pelvic mass and ascites.
  • For suspected cancer pattern, follow CA125 and ultrasound triage sequence.

heading: Primary care management
html:
The default approach is risk-stratified management with explicit safety-netting.

  • Cancer triage: check CA125 in women with suggestive symptoms; if CA125 is 35 IU/ml or higher, arrange ultrasound.
  • Urgent cancer referral: if ascites or suspicious pelvic/abdominal mass is present, or ultrasound suggests ovarian cancer.
  • Conservative management: for likely benign, simple, low-risk cysts, provide symptom control and interval review.
  • Premenopausal women: many simple/functional cysts resolve; refer if persistent symptoms, growth, or uncertain imaging.
  • Postmenopausal women: lower threshold for specialist risk review; simple unilateral unilocular cysts 3 cm or less are often low-risk and may not need routine follow-up.
  • Safety-net: advise urgent re-attendance for worsening pain, distension progression, systemic illness, or reduced oral intake.

heading: Who to refer
html:
Urgent same-day referral

  • Suspected ovarian torsion, rupture with instability, peritonitis, or sepsis.
  • Severe uncontrolled acute pelvic pain requiring urgent specialist assessment.

Urgent suspected cancer pathway referral

  • Ascites and/or pelvic or abdominal mass not clearly uterine fibroids.
  • Ultrasound features suspicious for ovarian malignancy.
  • High ongoing suspicion despite non-diagnostic first-line testing.

Routine referral

  • Persistent symptomatic cysts, enlarging lesions, or unresolved cysts on interval imaging.
  • Uncertain diagnosis or need for specialist management planning.

Include in referral

  • Menopausal status, symptom duration and progression.
  • CA125 result, ultrasound report, and key examination findings.
  • Pain burden, analgesic response, and safety-net actions already taken.

heading: Who not to refer
html:
Do not routinely refer asymptomatic or minimally symptomatic women with clearly low-risk simple cyst appearances and no concerning clinical features, when a documented conservative plan and safety-net are in place.

Continue primary care review with escalation if symptoms persist, worsen, or imaging risk profile changes.

heading: References
html:
• NICE NG12: suspected cancer referral (ovarian cancer section)
  • RCOG Green-top 62: suspected ovarian masses in premenopausal women
  • RCOG Green-top 34: ovarian cysts in postmenopausal women
